question: Is the overall visual style consistent with a scientific art illustration, combining artistic rendering with anatomical accuracy?
Does the foreground feature a Penrose triangle depicted as a solid object with its characteristic impossible geometry?
Is an Escher-style staircase depicted in the foreground, showing steps that paradoxically ascend or descend in an infinite loop?
Does the Müller-Lyer illusion in the foreground clearly show two line segments of equal length with opposing arrowheads (inward vs. outward fins)?
Are all three optical illusions (Penrose triangle, Escher staircase, Müller-Lyer) positioned in the foreground of the composition?
Does the background display a giant anatomical cross-section of a human eye relative to the other elements?
Are the specific anatomical parts of the eye (cornea, crystalline lens, and retina) clearly distinguishable in the cross-section?
Are light rays depicted passing through the cornea and lens to converge/form an image on the retina, consistent with optical physics?
Is the optic nerve bundle depicted extending posteriorly from the back of the eye toward the brain?
Does the optic nerve connect to the occipital lobe (visual cortex) at the posterior part of the brain?
Is the occipital lobe region of the brain highlighted using heat map colors (e.g., warm colors like red/orange) to indicate active zones?
